metadata.description: Not used, but needed to pass arm-ttk test `Location-Should-Not-Be-Hardcoded`.  We instead use the `workspace-location` which is derived from the LA workspace
[concat('Region to deploy solution resources -- separate from location selection',parameters('location'))]
Workspace name for Log Analytics where Microsoft Sentinel is setup

properties.description: XdrLogRaider data connector with template version 3.0.2

connectorUiConfig.title: Microsoft Defender XDR (XdrLogRaider)
connectorUiConfig.descriptionMarkdown: Open-source Microsoft Sentinel data connector for Microsoft Defender XDR portal-internal `/apiproxy/*` audit and reporting endpoints. Self-hosted in your Azure subscription with cookie-based authentication and transparent SSO refresh. Read-only by design — no action verbs are ever invoked on the Defender portal.

sampleQueries.query:
Defender_AnalyticsData_CL
| where TimeGenerated > ago(24h)
| project TimeGenerated, Operation, Subcategory, CorrelationId
| take 100

Defender_AnalyticsData_CL
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| summarize Count = count() by Operation
| order by Count desc

Defender_AttackSimulation_CL
| where TimeGenerated > ago(24h)
| project TimeGenerated, Operation, Subcategory, CorrelationId
| take 100

Defender_AttackSimulation_CL
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)

resourceProvider.permissionsDisplayText: read and write permissions on the workspace are required.

read permissions to shared keys for the workspace are required. [See the documentation to learn more about workspace keys](https://docs.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/platform/agent-windows#obtain-workspace-id-and-key).

customs.name: Microsoft.Web/sites permissions
customs.description: Read and write permissions to Azure Functions to create a Function App is required. [See the documentation to learn more about Azure Functions](https://docs.microsoft.com/azure/azure-functions/).

customs.name: Defender XDR service account
customs.description: An Entra service account with appropriate Defender XDR portal read permissions. The account satisfies MFA via TOTP or Passkey.

instructionSteps.description:
Use this method for automated deployment of the XdrLogRaider connector.

1. Click the **Deploy to Azure** button below.

	[![Deploy To Azure](https://aka.ms/deploytoazurebutton)](https://portal.azure.com/#create/Microsoft.Template/uri/https%3A%2F%2Fraw.githubusercontent.com%2FAzure%2FAzure-Sentinel%2Fmaster%2FSolutions%2FXdrLogRaider%2FData%2520Connectors%2Fazuredeploy_XdrLogRaider_FunctionApp.json)
2. Select the preferred **Subscription**, **Resource Group** and **Region**.
3. Provide the **Service account UPN**, **password**, and either the **TOTP secret** or **Passkey PEM**, and select your **Sentinel-enabled Log Analytics workspace**.
4. Review and click **Create**. The template deploys a self-hosted Function App plus its Key Vault, storage, data collection endpoint, custom tables and rules.
instructionSteps.title: Option 1 - Azure Resource Manager (ARM) Template

instructionSteps.description: The deployment template stored your service account credentials (password and TOTP seed or Passkey PEM) into the deployment Key Vault. No manual action is required if the deployment completed successfully.
instructionSteps.title: Step 1 · Service account credentials (automatic)

instructionSteps.description: On the first cycle, the Function App performs an unattended Entra OAuth login using the stored credentials. Single sign-on state is captured and persisted so subsequent cycles use silent refresh. No operator interaction is required.
instructionSteps.title: Step 2 · Unattended authentication (automatic)

instructionSteps.description: Query the workspace for ingested rows in any active `Defender_<Category>_CL` table. Cycle telemetry (Boot, Auth, Cycle, Entry events) is available via Application Insights for end-to-end observability.
instructionSteps.title: Step 3 · Verify ingestion (~10 minutes after deploy)

value:
union isfuzzy=true Defender_AnalyticsData_CL, Defender_AttackSimulation_CL, Defender_CloudApps_CL, Defender_Configuration_CL, Defender_EndpointManagement_CL, Defender_ExposureManagement_CL, Defender_Identity_CL, Defender_Operations_CL, Defender_PortalServices_CL, Defender_SecureScore_CL, Defender_VulnerabilityManagement_CL
| summarize LastLogReceived = max(TimeGenerated)
| project IsConnected = LastLogReceived > ago(3d)

properties.descriptionHtml:
Note: Please refer to the following before installing the solution:

• Review the solution Release Notes

• There may be known issues pertaining to this Solution, please refer to them before installing.

The XdrLogRaider solution is an open-source, community data connector for Microsoft Defender XDR portal-internal /apiproxy/* audit and reporting endpoints. It ingests Defender XDR events into per-Category custom Log Analytics tables (Defender_<Category>_CL) with one typed row per event and the raw JSON preserved, using dynamic per-tenant capability discovery.

Underlying Microsoft technologies used:

  1. Azure Functions (self-hosted PowerShell Function App in your subscription)

  2. Data Collection Rules and Endpoints

NOTE: This connector authenticates to and reads from Microsoft Defender XDR portal-internal /apiproxy/* endpoints, which are undocumented and unsupported and may change or break without notice. XdrLogRaider is a community, non-Microsoft project; it is read-only by design and intended for authorized use within your own tenant. Authentication uses a dedicated Entra service account with TOTP or Passkey MFA and transparent SSO refresh. See the project repository for the full data-source, authentication, and security model.

Data Connectors: 1

Learn more about Microsoft Sentinel | Learn more about Solutions
